title: Psychotria biaurita
taxonomy: Psychotria biaurita is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Rubioideae subfamily. Psychotria biaurita is a member of the Rubiaceae family, specifically within the Rubiaceae subfamily of Rubioideae. The species is native to Central and South America.

intro: Psychotria biaurita, also known as the "Brazilian yellow psychotria," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria biaurita include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
